meta_domains_consumers: internal/validate/plan.go:735 loadImpactedDomains -> checkADRCoverage:520 / checkADRCitations:480 (iterates by NAME, no file attribution; untouched by R1's chosen mechanism)
internal/contextpack/budgeter.go:180 (context-pack domain list; reads names; untouched)
internal/validate/docsync.go (does NOT read meta.Domains — already uses resolveDomains over ALL domains; R1 only brings divergence into line with doc-sync)
blocking: R1 text and In-Scope name the 'plan adr-coverage attribution path' and list internal/contextpack/spec.go (ParseSpec) as a changed file, but neither needs to change. checkADRCoverage/checkADRCitations iterate the literal Impacted-Domains strings BY NAME and do no file-attribution/glob-match at all; and the R1 Design Decision explicitly RETAINS ParseSpec output unchanged ('the Impacted Domains list is retained as advisory scope'). So (a) 'and the plan adr-coverage attribution path MUST glob-match' describes a path that does not exist, and (b) spec.go is in In-Scope for a change R1's own design says must NOT happen. Either remove spec.go from In-Scope (R1 is a one-site divergence.go change) and drop the 'plan adr-coverage attribution path' clause, OR — if a file-path Impacted-Domains entry is meant to stop poisoning checkADRCoverage (it currently iterates 'genevieve/review.py' as a bogus domain and emits adr-coverage-missing) — say THAT explicitly as a requirement, because it is a real, separate behavior change the current text neither mandates nor tests.
summary: R1 blast radius is BOUNDED: the only authoritative reader of meta.Domains-as-resolution-key is divergence.go:142; doc-sync already enumerates all domains via resolveDomains (so R1 makes divergence match doc-sync, not diverge), and budgeter/domain-show/plan-coverage all read meta.Domains as a NAME list that R1's design explicitly preserves. Exclusions are honored in requirement text, not just asserted: #146 and #76 are named OUT with 'separate spec' rationale, R3 is test-only ('This requirement MUST NOT modify the ADR parser') and Out-of-Scope repeats it, parser already accepts both Domain(s) forms. Decomposition is clean: R1 (divergence.go candidate-set) / R2 (plan.go:529 message) / R3 (cmd adr.go root + parse_test) / R4 (approve scaffold + plan.go:141 message) are independent — no hidden coupling (R2's message edit does not depend on R1's attribution; they touch different functions). Right-sizing is sensible: bundling the three #145 friction fixes with the #147 attribution fix is coherent (all four are 'make the ownership/ADR gate satisfiable honestly'), and R1 is a contained ~1-site change, not its own spec. The ONE genuine creep is the over-broad In-Scope/R1 wording pulling ParseSpec (spec.go) into a change its own Design Decision forbids, plus a phantom 'plan adr-coverage attribution path'. Tighten that to: R1 = divergence.go candidate-set only (drop spec.go from In Scope), or make the checkADRCoverage file-path-poisoning fix an explicit, tested requirement. CHANGES on under-specified/over-scoped R1 surface, not on blast radius (which is bounded) and not on decomposition (which is clean).
